title: SQLite pool setup: PRAGMAs, WAL mode, connection validation
description:
Create the SQLite connection pool using sqlx::SqlitePool. Apply all required PRAGMAs on every new connection via the after_connect hook: journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000, cache_size=-64000. Verify PRAGMAs are actually applied (don't assume — query PRAGMA journal_mode after setting it).

Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to study sqlx SqliteConnectOptions and after_connect hook API. Spawn a testing subagent to write concurrency tests.

Test discipline:
- Open pool, query 'PRAGMA journal_mode' — must return 'wal'
- Query 'PRAGMA foreign_keys' — must return '1'
- Test that inserting a row that violates FK constraint returns sqlx error (not silent ignore)
- Concurrent write test: spawn 10 tokio tasks each doing 100 inserts, verify no data loss (busy_timeout prevents deadlock)

Defensive programming:
- Validate the database file can be opened before starting the HTTP server
- On connection failure: log the error with the database path, return clear startup error
- Never store the pool in a global static — pass as axum State
acceptance_criteria: All PRAGMAs verified by query after connection; FK violations return errors; 10 concurrent writers complete without loss; database path validated at startup
